rules.text: tutor must not write outside its own working directory
tutor must not request elevated privileges
tutor must not reach the network
do not introduce dependencies outside bash and a standard POSIX userland
Lessons must be runnable end-to-end on Linux or macOS without prerequisite installation beyond bash
anything that requires a specific username, hostname, distro, or shell theme is a portability bug

embedded_policy_findings.policy_home_proposed: static-analysis fitness function (FF-001 / FF-002 in Phase 10); consistent with project's bash-only constraint
embedded_policy_findings.drift_target: any future change to shelltutor that violates one of the six rules

citations.evidence: Do not introduce dependencies that require installation outside bash and a standard POSIX userland
tutor must not write outside its own working directory, must not request elevated privileges, and must not reach the network

contradictory_guidance.summary: STATUS.md and ROADMAP.md claim user-agnostic refactor is pending; commit e6257aa shows it complete (Phase 1 finding). Surfaces as documentation-as-policy drift at the skill-or-sop layer.

citations.evidence: User-agnostic refactor commit ... Immediate Next Step #1
no WYN OPS reference in current script

tool_description_policy.reason: no tools in the agent-runtime sense

untrusted_input_boundary.reason: Learner input goes to a real bash subshell, not into model context. No privileged prompt context exists.

server_exposed_prompts.reason: no MCP / A2A / protocol-advertised prompt surface

evidence_in_phase_8: Embedded policy in AGENTS.md without code-level enforcement (POL-001)
Documentation drift between STATUS/ROADMAP and commit log (referred to forecast F-001)

reference_doc_observation.role: Operator-supplied curriculum reference for a hypothetical Shell Foundations for Vimtutor course. Not project authority. Phase 1 already noted vocabulary subset is intentional per README non-goals 67-75. No Phase 8 finding derives from this comparison.
